name: Mordovia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the dominant colour of Mordvin national costume
purity, wisdom and the cleanliness of the Mordvin soul
the earth, motherhood and the rivers Sura and Moksha

description: A horizontal triband with a narrow red top and bottom and a wide white centre stripe in the proportion 1:3:1; in the centre of the white stripe is a red eight-pointed Mordvin solar sign.
history: Adopted on 30 March 1995 by the State Assembly of the Republic of Mordovia.

features.symbolism: red recalls the principal colour of Mordvin folk costume; white stands for purity, wisdom and the cleanliness of the Mordvin soul; dark blue represents the earth, the Sura and Moksha rivers, and motherhood.
an eight-pointed solar emblem, a traditional Mordvin sign of the sun, life and well-being.
